titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Gatianus
VL30
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Mt Mc Lc Jo

surrogates.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers ff. 85r-109r. Although words are normally separated, there are some instances in which proclitic prepositions and et are attached (e.g. delumine, apatre).The transcription includes foliation, lineation, all punctuation, capitalisation, rubrication (where discernable) and abbreviations. The unusual word division has not been recorded. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Change to verse division of 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: This folio contains the end of Luke, together with an explicit
Decorative pattern to end of line
Highly ornate and stylized display page
Sense break marked with x by corrector
There is a mark above 'uolumptate' which may be a correction.
The alternative has been added above the line.
'in il(lo tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
An 'e' has been written over the 'e' of 'propter'; the reason for this is not clear.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
One or two letters may have been erased here.
There are two dots in the m of 'aquam' which may be a deletion.
'in il(lo tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
'i¬hs dix(it)'; lectionary indication in left margin.
The word 'unicum' is added in the right margin, apparently by the first hand, with a sign indicating its position in the line.
Hole in parchment extending over five lines.
Lectionary indication above line, illegible on our photos.
Hole in parchment extending over five lines.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
'uos' may be rubricated.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
The alternative has been added above the line.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
The 'u' and 'b' are also written above the word as well as corrections to the letters themselves.
'i(n) ill(o tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
Hole in parchment extending over 3 lines.
Hole in parchment extending over 3 lines.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
Unclear lectionary indication above line; probably 'in il(lo tempore)'.
'in ill(o tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
One or two characters may have been erased after 'enim'.
Lectionary indication above line; probably 'ih¬s d(i)x(it)'.
Lectionary indication above line; probably 'ih¬s d(i)x(it)'.
'in ill(o tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
Ammonian section number appears in text rather than in margin.
'in il(lo tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
'il(lo tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line; there is also a large letter Z in margin.
'uel iste' added above line, probably by first hand.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
'uel iudei' added above line, probably by first hand.
Lectionary indications above line, in order to read: in ill(o tempore) perrexit ih¬s'
'ill(o tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
'ill(o tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line..
There is a stroke on the bottom of the 'n', suggesting that 'superniis' may be the intended reading.
Illegible mark above line, probably connected with lectionary.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
Hole in parchment.
'uel cognoscetis' added above line.
'in ill(o tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
The alternative is written above line probably by first hand
Lectionary indications added in order to read: in ill(o tempore) praeteriens ih¬s'
'uel tibi' is added above the line by the first hand.
Possible faint correction to 'ergo'
The rest of the letters on this page are written in slightly smaller script.
'in ill(o tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
'in ill(o tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
There is a hole in the parchment: although the copyist began by writing 'iohan', after the hole he wrote 'annis'.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication in left-hand margin.
Hole in parchment.
Hole in parchment.
Hole in parchment.
Hole in parchment.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
'ill(o tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
There is a gap in 'qua', which may originally have read 'quia'.
A letter may have been erased after 'ueni'.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication in margin.
Knot design in bottom right hand corner of page.
There is an illegible ink mark between 'et' and 'cum'.
'il(lo tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
'il(lo tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
Ink mark above final 't' of 'uenit'
Ink mark above final 't' of 'uenit'
'il(lo tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
Correction marked by cross and added at foot of page; the hand is possibly that of the original scribe.
Correction continues at foot of page.
'in ill(o tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
Correction added in right margin, possibly by first hand.
'il(lo tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
The correction is very faint.
'il(lo tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
The Ammonian section number is written above the line rather than in the margin.
'eorum', possibly written by the first hand, projects into the right margin, which suggests that is is an addition.
'il(lo tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
'il(lo tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line.
There is a hole in the parchment at the beginning of the line.
'il(lo tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication above line. The following 15 lines have had numerous punctuation marks added to aid reading. These have not been recorded.
The mark before 'dicebant' appears to indicate the start of a new sentence; seen the note on verse 16 above.
The mark before 'Cognouit' appears to indicate the start of a new sentence; seen the note on verse 16 above.
A word may have been erased here.
Cross added by later hand above line to signify end of lection
'in il(lo tempore) d(i)x(it)'; lectionary indication in right margin
Hole in parchment.
Lectionary indications above line, in order to read: 'in ill(o tempore) subleuatis ih¬s oculis'.
This may be intended to be an alternative reading, 'omne quod' or 'omnes quos', but there is no 'uel' and it is not clear whether the correction is to 'omne' or 'omnes'.
uenio divided over two lines, 'ue / nio'; a later hand has unnecessarily added -nio at the end of the upper of the two lines.
There is a long addition above the line, illegible in our images.
Lectionary indications above line, in order to read: 'in ill(o tempore) egresus est ih¬s'.
The alternative is added above the line, consisting of the abbreviation for 'uel' and an 'e' above the 'a' of 'malchus'
Small superlinear note, illegible from photographs
Hole in parchment.
Hole in parchment.
Hole in parchment.
sic
The correction is added above the line, possibly by the first hand.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
'in ill(o tempore)'; lectionary indication above line.
Lectionary indications above line, in order to read: 'in ill(o tempore) dicit ih¬s'.
There is a dot either side of 'ih&nasalbar;s', presumably so that it is omitted in the lectionary reading (which has added the word earlier in the phrase).
Lectionary indications above line, in order to read: 'in ill(o tempore) dixit ih¬s petro'.
Large cross, apparently not first hand, presumably marking end of lection
Decorative pattern of about 30 diples to end of line.

There is an eight-line inscription written by the first hand, which is too faint to make out in full from our images. The visible portion of the first line reads something like: 'uolcundus mihi trinitas misser..', and the second line includes the words 'uos omnes xp¬iani ut pro me...'.
On the final line of the page a much more recent hand has written 'mo(na)sterii s(ancti) Zenonis maioris Verona'.
